subtasks.description: Fetch Adobe license data from MongoDB including subscription tiers, entitlements, contract terms, and license counts
Query Snowflake for Adobe Q4 usage metrics including consumption, cost, and product breakdown
Reconcile MongoDB license state with Snowflake Q4 usage; identify over/under-utilization, cost variance, and renewal posture; generate summary for Slack posting
Post reconciliation summary and recommendations to #sam-team Slack channel

plan.cost_rationale: 4 subtasks (minimal decomposition): mongo_query and sql_gen run in parallel (no dependency) to fetch both data sources simultaneously; cross_recon (mid tier) handles the complex reconciliation of two sources with variance analysis and posture classification—this is inherently cross_recon work, not a separate policy action; multitool_plan (small) orchestrates the Slack post via the 25-tool registry. MongoDB and SQL queries are small tier (straightforward data fetch). Cross_recon is mid tier because it must reconcile two distinct sources, compute variances, and generate actionable recommendations. Total tool calls: 2 (parallel fetches) + 1 (reconcile) + 1 (Slack post) = 4 calls, well under the 6-call budget.

output.sql: SELECT fiscal_year, fiscal_quarter, publisher, product, event_type, COUNT(*) as event_count, SUM(amount_usd) as total_cost_usd, SUM(daily_active_seats) as total_seats, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) as unique_users FROM consumption_facts WHERE publisher = 'Adobe' AND fiscal_quarter = 4 GROUP BY fiscal_year, fiscal_quarter, publisher, product, event_type ORDER BY fiscal_year DESC, product

arguments.message:
📊 **Adobe License Reconciliation Summary - Q4 2024**

**Overview:**
Reconciled 4 Adobe product lines with usage data. Key findings below:

**1. Adobe Creative Cloud Q4 2024** ✅ STABLE
• Licenses: 150 | Active Users: 138 | Utilization: 94.7%
• Cost: $67,500 | Per-Seat Cost: $450
• Status: Under-provisioned by 8 seats (-5.3%)
• Contract: Active until 2025-03-31 (Pending renewal)
• **Recommendation:** Monitor utilization; consider 8-seat increase at renewal

**2. Adobe Document Cloud Q4 2024** ⚠️ REDUCE
• Licenses: 80 | Active Users: 62 | Utilization: 81.3%
• Cost: $28,900 | Per-Seat Cost: $361.25
• Status: Over-provisioned by 15 seats (-18.75%)
• Contract: **EXPIRED** (2024-12-31)
• **Recommendation:** URGENT - Renegotiate/renew immediately. Reduce to 65 seats to optimize spend

**3. Adobe Experience Cloud Q4 2024** 📈 HOLD
• Licenses: 45 | Active Users: 43 | Utilization: 97.8%
• Cost: $51,300 | Per-Seat Cost: $1,140 (highest)
• Status: Optimized (-1 seat variance, -2.22%)
• Contract: Active until 2025-06-30
• **Recommendation:** Hold current allocation; high utilization and value justify premium cost

**4. Adobe Stock Q4 2024** 🔄 RENEWAL DECISION PENDING
• Licenses: 200 | Active Users: 26 | Utilization: 14%
• Cost: $14,200 | Significant over-provisioning (172 unused seats)
• Contract: Renewal decision point (2025-01-15)
• **Recommendation:** Negotiate down to ~30 seats; 86% cost savings potential

**Summary Metrics:**
• Total Portfolio Cost: $161,900
• Total Licenses Provisioned: 375
• Total Active Users: 269
• Overall Utilization: 71.7%
• Optimization Opportunity: ~$12,000+ annual savings

**Action Items:**
1. [URGENT] Renew Adobe Document Cloud before contract expires
2. Reduce Adobe Stock licenses at upcoming renewal
3. Plan Creative Cloud expansion for Q1 2025
4. Monitor Experience Cloud ROI (highest cost/seat)

—Generated from reconciliation audit
